results.rendered_html:
daśārṇa gaṇa vimuktādi zu  P. 5,2,61 .
1〉 m. pl. N. pr. eines Volkes im Südosten von Madhyadeśa  VARĀH. BṚH. S. 5,40 . 10,15 . 14,10 . 16,26 . 31,11 .  MBH. 1,4449 . 2,1063 . 1189 . 4,12 . 144 . 6,348 . 350 . 362  ( VP. 186 . 187 . 192 ). 14,2470 .  N. 17,13 . 14 .  HARIV. 1203 . 4967 .  R. 4,41,14 .  MEGH. 24 . [Page3-0555] sg. der König der Daś.  MBH. 5,7519 . daśārṇo deśaḥ  P. 6,1,89, Vārtt. 7 , Sch.  DURGĀD. _zu_ VOP. 2,9 . Soll aus daśan + ṛṇa zusammengesetzt sein.
— 2〉 f. ā N. pr. eines im Vindhya entspringenden Flusses  VP. 185, N. 80 .  DURGĀD. _zu_ VOP. 2,9 .  ŚABDĀRTHAKALPATARU _im_ ŚKDR. 
— Vgl. dāśārṇa.

results.rendered_html: daśārṇa N. of a country, through which flows the Daśārṇā (Dasan). It was the eastern part of Mālava or Mālvā, its capital being Vidiśā-the modern Bhilsā-situated on the Vetravatī or Betva, cf.  Me. 24 , 25  and Kādambarī. Kālidāsa also makes Vidiśā a river which is probably the same as the Bees that joins the Betva.
